---
title: "Old RStan Instruction "
editor: 
  mode: source
---

{{< include ../_macros.qmd >}}

## Outline

For reference, we keep here the old instructions for RStan. 
They may still 
work on some plate-forms. But we are now migrating to 
[cmdstanr](../w08_mcmc1/topic01_installing_stan.qmd).



## Installing (old RStan method)

There are two main steps to install Stan:

1. [Configuring a C++ Toolchain](https://github.com/stan-dev/rstan/wiki/RStan-Getting-Started#configuring-c-toolchain)
2. [Installing RStan](https://github.com/stan-dev/rstan/wiki/RStan-Getting-Started#installing-rstan)


## Running Stan (old RStan method)

Download the [following templates](https://github.com/UBC-Stat-ML/web447/raw/main/blocks/stan_templates.zip) which you can 
use as a starting point for either R script or notebook.

For example:

```r
require(rstan)
fit = stan(
  seed = 123,
  file = "../w08_mcmc1/beta_binomial.stan",  # Stan program
  data = list(n=3, k=3),        # named list of data
  iter = 1000                   # number of samples to draw
```


## Extracting draws

```r
samples = extract(fit)
```

## Histograms with bayesplots

```r
mcmc_hist(fit)
```
